items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update resolves multiple vulnerabilities with severity ratings up to CVSS 8.7, which could allow attackers to trigger application denial-of-service, exploit vulnerable third-party dependencies, escalate privileges, and bypass security mechanisms. Adobe recommends applying the latest security patch immediately to protect against severe threats.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento full-page cache extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. The flaw is rated 9.8 (critical) and has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered the PolyShell vulnerability, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, bypassing validation. This can lead to remote code execution or account takeover, with mass exploitation observed since March 19, 2026.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This security update addresses multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization leading to Full System Takeover
items.summary: Part of APSB26-05, this critical vulnerability allows attackers to execute code remotely, potentially leading to a full system takeover due to incorrect authorization.

items.title: Improper Access Control leading to Data Leak
items.summary: Addressed in APSB26-05, this critical improper access control vulnerability can bypass security filters to access restricted information, resulting in a data leak.

items.title: Stored XSS leading to Admin Hijacking
items.summary: This critical stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability, resolved in APSB26-05, allows malicious scripts to escalate user privileges and potentially hijack administrator accounts.
